day1.question: Roof Ignite operates on what billing model?
options: Performance-based 28-day cycles — client pays ONLY after targets are hit
Weekly billing based on ad spend

day1.question: A client is spending $100/day. Target is 15 booked appointments. What is the minimum to bill them?
What is the standard retainer structure today?
When does a client pay their retainer?
options: After the cycle completes and targets are met

day1.question: Which three niches does Roof Ignite serve?
day1.explanation: Roofing (80-90% of revenue), HVAC, and Gutters. Roofing is the primary focus with the highest average ticket size.

day2.question: Which tool is used for task management at Roof Ignite?
When should you handle A2P registration yourself?
options: Never — always assign to Emmanuel via A2P Wizard

day2.question: What are the only allowed Meta ad placements?
options: Facebook Feed, IG Feed, FB Stories, IG Stories, FB Reels, IG Reels (6 only)

day2.question: How do you get Logbook access?
After doing one training setup, what is your ongoing role for account setups?
options: Task all setups to Emmanuel via ClickUp — never set up again
Set up first 5 accounts, then hand off

day2.question: Ken is based in the Philippines. What is his expected turnaround time?
What does Mervin do at Roof Ignite?
options: Full-cycle media buyer and setup support — similar role to Emmanuel
day2.explanation: Mervin is a full-cycle media buyer who supports Emmanuel on setups and ongoing media buying tasks. Task him via ClickUp just as you would Emmanuel.

day3.question: During a setup audit, call recording in GHL should be:
You're stuck on a GHL setting during your training setup. First step?
options: Open another live client sub-account and see how it's configured

day3.question: What does the Account Specific Document contain?
options: VA cheat sheet — service area, hours, owner name, calendar login, lead Q&A
List of all clients across all pods

day3.question: After an onboarding call, what's the correct sequence within 1 hour?
options: Build the client's GHL sub-account yourself
Paste Fathom transcript into Custom GPT → post to #post-onboarding-discussion → ClickUp task for Emmanuel → text client
Send a Loom of their ads

day3.question: You should NEVER create a Facebook Business Manager for a client. Why?
options: Creates legal liability — BMs must be owned by the client, not us

day3.question: Which Slack channel do you post to after every onboarding call?
day3.explanation: #post-onboarding-discussion receives your Custom GPT summary from every onboarding call. This keeps Jonathan and the team looped in immediately after every new client call.

day4.question: What must every Monday/Thursday status update include?
options: Per account: name, cycle #, days elapsed, bookings vs target, health color, notes, and action steps WITH dates

day4.question: Pod managers post updates Monday/Thursday. When are review calls?
Layer 1 metrics are great for an account. Should you audit Meta KPIs?
options: Yes — weekly audit for all accounts
No — if Layer 1 is healthy, check in monthly only

day4.question: A client's ads have been paused 3 days due to billing failure. What do you do?
options: CALL the client immediately — every paused day = delayed cycle and cashflow
Restart from a different ad account

day4.question: When should you initiate the renewal conversation?
day4.explanation: Initiate renewal 5–7 days before cycle end. Never let a cycle expire without already having confirmed the next one. Proactive renewals feel like a win; surprise renewals create friction.

week2.question: You receive 5 accounts in the handoff. Which do you prioritize first?
options: Healthiest accounts first — protect the wins
Launch-pending → underperforming → healthy (healthy = monthly check-in only)

week2.question: A client says their close rate on appointments is low. First step?
options: Request full CRM sales data — what happened to each appointment?
Offer a discount on next cycle

week2.question: The 24–48 hour rule means:
options: Respond to all Slacks in 24–48 hours
If a client owes you something, get an answer within 24–48 hours or escalate
New clients get a Loom within 24–48 hours
Renewals confirmed 24–48 hours before cycle end

week2.question: What is the KRS (Klaus Roofing Systems) retainer rate?
After a successful cycle, what revenue activities should you always do?
options: Request a video testimonial, ask for referrals, confirm cycle renewal proactively
Wait for the client to bring up testimonials organically
Offer a discount to lock in next cycle

week2.question: Leila's title is:
week2.explanation: Leila is Head of VA Management — the senior VA manager who leads all VAs. Aica supports her. Leila also controls Logbook access.

final.question: What is the current company revenue and the target?
What does the 10% of ad spend in the retainer structure pay for?
options: Scalability — higher spend = more leads, more VA time, more management. Incentivizes us to scale clients.

final.question: Which ad creative type performs BEST for roofing clients?
options: Raw client photos, Canva trifolds, Ken's AI images — native-looking content

final.question: A client has a Facebook page not in their Business Manager. Solution?
options: Create a new page from their BM
Tell them you can't run ads
Have client add you as individual with "Run Ads" access via page Settings (requires FB friend request)
Create a Business Manager for them

final.question: What is the Logbook?
options: Central truth for ALL lead data — every lead from every account documented by VAs with status and notes

final.question: When should you look at Layer 2 metrics (CPC, CTR, conversion rate)?
options: Only when Layer 1 metrics are failing
Only in week 1 of a campaign

final.question: What is the first thing you do when an onboarding call lands on your calendar?
options: Pull closer call recording, research client, send pre-onboarding email + text, create ClickUp task for Emmanuel
Ask Jonathan to join as support

final.question: You're on Day 42 of a cycle and still 2 appointments short of the 80% rule. What happens?
options: Continue extending at no charge and escalate strategy to Jonathan immediately

final.question: What is the Speed to Cashflow target?
options: 120 days from signed contract to first payment
final.explanation: The company average is 75–80 days from signed contract to first payment. Your target is 60 or less. Every day a client isn't launched = delayed cashflow for you and the business.

final.question: What is the ICP (Ideal Client Profile) for Roof Ignite?
options: Any contractor with a Facebook account
Startups under 1 year old with high growth ambition
$2.5M+ revenue, 3+ years in business, growth-minded, $3K+/month ad spend capacity

final.question: How do you handle a survey with low conversion (lots of leads, few bookings)?
options: Increase ad budget to get more leads
Audit survey questions — remove friction, test shorter form, check if qualifier questions are filtering too hard or too soft
Escalate to Oscar without reviewing yourself

final.question: What does the Command Center show that turns RED?
options: Accounts behind on bookings vs. target — your daily triage dashboard

final.question: What are the three niches Roof Ignite serves, and what is the primary one?
options: Plumbing, HVAC, Roofing — Plumbing is primary
Roofing (80–90%), HVAC, Gutters — Roofing is primary
Roofing, Solar, Landscaping — Roofing is primary
HVAC, Electrical, Roofing — HVAC is primary

final.question: What is the 24–48 hour rule?
options: All ClickUp tasks must be completed within 48 hours
You have 24–48 hours to identify and resolve any client blocker, or escalate — no blocker sits unaddressed
Client calls must be returned within 24 hours
New accounts must go live within 48 hours of signing
